Data Scientist - RevOps Careers

# Data Scientist

On Site Full Time Malaysia ExxonMobil

ExxonMobil is seeking a Data Scientist to join their Kuala Lumpur team. The successful candidate will work on developing and deploying data science solutions to improve business performance and drive innovation. The role is based in Kuala Lumpur and offers a competitive benefits package.

## Requirements

- Post a Job
- Master’s, or PhD degree in Data Science, Computer Science, IT, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, or related disciplines
- At least 3 years of experience in developing, applying, and validating data-driven tools to model complex systems
- In-depth knowledge and practical experience in statistical analysis techniques and machine learning techniques
- Practical experience in the full machine learning lifecycle, from problem formulation and data acquisition to model building and deployment at enterprise scale
- Deep conceptual and mathematical understanding of algorithms, models, model assessment techniques, solution development and explainable AI

## Benefits

- Competitive benefits package
- Opportunity to work on exciting and challenging projects
- Collaborative and dynamic work environment
- Professional development opportunities

To apply for this job please visit jobs.exxonmobil.com.

results.title: Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil | Future Digital Twin
highlights:
Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil | Future Digital Twin

# Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil

Cristi Nastase of ExxonMobil at Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 explored how enterprise architecture must adapt for the rise of agentic AI, with a focus on trust, governance, explainability, and intelligent workflows at scale.

- Future Digital Twin
- May 14, 2026

GO BACK

## Enterprise Architecture in the Age of Agentic AI

Cristi Nastase, ExxonMobil

Cristi Nastase, Sr. Principal Enterprise Architect – GenAI at ExxonMobil, discusses how enterprise architecture must evolve as agentic AI moves from experimentation into operational reality.

The presentation explores how the centre of gravity in AI is rapidly shifting from traditional machine learning toward GenAI and agentic AI, while governance, controls, and operational discipline struggle to keep pace. As organisations push beyond experimentation, ExxonMobil highlights the growing need for enterprise ecosystems that can support AI at scale without creating fragmentation, uncontrolled sprawl, or “pilot purgatory”.

A major focus of the session is trust and explainability. Nastase argues that organisations must move beyond “black box” AI by designing systems with verifiable lineage, auditability, and policy-driven controls built in from the start. In practice, this means enabling operators to understand not only what an AI agent recommends, but also the data, models, and logic behind every decision.

The session also challenges organisations to rethink where real competitive advantage will come from in the age of agentic AI. Rather than relying solely on foundation models, ExxonMobil emphasises the importance of intelligent workflows, proprietary operational data, strong governance, and human judgement capable of asking the right contextual questions. The message is clear: enterprise AI success depends as much on architecture and organisational readiness as it does on the technology itself.

results.title: Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil | Future Digital Twin
highlights:
Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil | Future Digital Twin

# Presentation: Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 – ExxonMobil

Chris Willmore of ExxonMobil at Future Digital Twin & AI USA 2026 explored how digital twins are being scaled across Guyana’s offshore operations, sharing practical lessons on architecture, execution, and building connected asset management at scale.

- Future Digital Twin
- May 14, 2026

GO BACK

## Digital Twins at Scale – Strategy, Execution and Challenges in Guyana

Chris Willmore, ExxonMobil

Explore how Chris Willmore, Digital Manager, Guyana Digital Alliance at ExxonMobil, is scaling digital twins across Guyana’s offshore operations and building the foundations for more connected and data-driven asset management.

The presentation examines what it takes to deliver digital twins in complex industrial environments, combining visual simulation, contextualised data, predictive capabilities, 3D models, and real-time operational information into a unified digital ecosystem. It also highlights the architecture supporting ExxonMobil’s Guyana operations across multiple FPSOs.

A major focus is the acceleration of digital twin delivery, with deployment times reduced from 14 months to under 3 months for future vessels through improved data modelling, automated pipelines, CI/CD processes, and scalable delivery practices.

The session also shares practical lessons around data quality, user experience, persona-led design, and maintaining accurate virtual representations of physical facilities. The message is clear: digital twins are rapidly becoming essential tools for engineering, maintenance, integrity, and reliability teams across the oil and gas sector.

highlights:
ExxonMobil Careers announces Chemical Engineering Jobs for Flow Assurance Engineer professionals in Bangalore.
[...]
## ExxonMobil Careers Hiring for Chemical Engineering Jobs in Bangalore
[...]
Searching for advanced Chemical Engineering Jobs in the global energy industry? ExxonMobil Careers is offering an exciting opportunity for experienced professionals through its Flow Assurance Engineer opening in Bangalore. This role is ideal for candidates interested in Oil and Gas Careers involving multiphase flow analysis, process modeling, production systems, and pipeline engineering while working with one of the world’s leading energy technology companies.
[...]
- Position Title: Flow Assurance Engineer
[...]
ExxonMobil Corporation is one of the world’s largest publicly traded energy and chemical companies focused on energy innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering solutions. The company operates across upstream, product solutions, and low-carbon businesses with a strong global presence. ExxonMobil Careers provide exceptional opportunities for professionals in engineering, technology, research, and oil and gas operations with exposure to world-class projects and advanced technical systems.
[...]
The Flow Assurance Engineer will support the design and operability assessment of oil and gas production systems, including wells, pipelines, flow lines, risers, and production facilities. The role includes fluid characterization, multiphase flow modeling, transient flow simulations, hydrate and wax deposition analysis, corrosion and erosion assessment, and development of operational
[...]
strategies for production systems. Candidates will work with advanced simulation and thermodynamic modeling software to optimize production efficiency and maintain operational safety standards.
[...]
- Steady-state, multiphase fluid modeling to design production systems, including functional requirements for artificial lift and/or thermal insulation
[...]
- Transient, multiphase fluid modeling to assess thermal and hydraulic transients during production rate changes, turndown, startup, shutdown, and blow-down; including flow line and riser slugging, cool-down, and warm-up.
[...]
- Use of modeling and/or laboratory analysis to predict and assess solid formation, deposition, bedding and/or blockage behavior for hydrates, sand, wax, scale, asphaltenes, and other solids.
[...]
Skills and Qualifications
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in engineering preferably in Chemical/Mechanical or Petroleum from a recognized university with CGPA 7 and above
- Minimum 3 years’ experience in Flow Assurance or Multiphase Flow Analysis in Oil and Gas
[...]
- Should have exposure to software like OLGA, PIPESIM, Prosper or GAP
[...]
- Willingness and ability to travel internationally
[...]
- Demonstrated work experience as a flow assurance engineer on an oil or gas field development project
[...]
- Demonstrated work experience in development of multicomponent fluid characterizations using a thermodynamic or process simulator, such as PVTSIM, HYSYS, or equivalent
[...]
Preferred Qualifications/Experience
[...]
- Formal training and demonstrated work experience in OLGA transient multiphase flow modeling
- Demonstrated work experience in prediction of gas hydrate formation and development of operational strategies for hydrate blockage prevention
- Demonstrated work experience in oil and gas process modeling, using HYSYS, GAP or equivalent
- Knowledge of Pipeline and Subsea engineering.
[...]
Professionals searching for high-impact Chemical Engineering Jobs in the energy industry should explore this exciting opportunity at ExxonMobil Careers. The Flow Assurance Engineer role offers exposure to advanced oil and gas technologies, multiphase flow systems, pipeline engineering, and global-scale production projects. Candidates passionate about innovation, engineering analysis, and Oil and Gas Careers can build a rewarding long-term career with ExxonMobil in Bangalore.

results.title: ExxonMobil says AI model identified Guyana discoveries with 90% success rate | OilNOW
highlights:
# ExxonMobil says AI model identified Guyana discoveries with 90% success rate
[...]
ExxonMobil says an artificial intelligence model built using Guyana’s offshore seismic data was able to identify already-discovered crude oil accumulations with a 90% success rate.
[...]
Neil Chapman, Senior Vice President of ExxonMobil, said the model forms part of the company’s wider use of artificial intelligence in oil and gas exploration. He made the comments during the Bernstein 42nd Annual Strategic Decisions Conference on May 28.
[...]
Chapman said artificial intelligence could help companies assess large volumes of subsurface data and identify patterns that may support future exploration.
[...]
He said ExxonMobil tested the model against discoveries already made offshore Guyana.
[...]
“…in Guyana, we have built an agent, a model…which if we give it the seismic data that we’ve run and we say, go find the crude oil, it can find all the crude oil that we’ve already found with a 90% success rate,” Chapman said.
[...]
Chapman said the company has also used artificial intelligence to review well data from across the industry.
[...]
“We have analyzed the well data from 50,000 wells that have been drilled in the industry all over the world, 50,000,” Chapman said. “It would have taken us 15 years to do that analysis. We’ve done it in a matter of weeks.”
[...]
Chapman said the work is aimed at finding similarities in subsurface data that may have been missed through conventional analysis. He said the review identified 150 exploration opportunities worldwide, but those opportunities still have to be tested by drilling.
[...]
Chapman cautioned that ExxonMobil has not fully solved the use of artificial intelligence in exploration.
[...]
“I don’t want to say we’ve reached the holy grail in AI and exploration, we certainly have not. But what I’m saying is we’re making progress,” Chapman said.

Research and build AI/ML systems for oil and gas applications including subsurface characterization and reservoir engineering at Occidental Petroleum's AI Center of Excellence in Houston.

## Full Job Description
[...]
We are looking for an experienced and innovative individual contributor to fill the position of **AI Machine Learning Engineer **within our **AI Center of Excellence** group based in **Houston, TX. **The incumbent will research, build, and design artificial intelligence systems to automate predictive models, and design machine learning systems, models and schemes. 
**Essential Job Responsibilities**
[...]
* Work with Subject Matter Experts to design appropriate machine learning model types and architectures for particular applications ranging from subsurface characterization, reservoir engineering, production, and facilities etc
* Tune algorithm hyper-parameters and perform model training and validation
* Create workflows to optimize performance for computationally heavy workloads
* Collaborate with Machine Learning Operation team to deploy machine learning solutions to the cloud
[...]
**Qualifications**
[...]
* Bachelor's degree in computer science, mathematics, physics, engineering or related field required
* Master's degree is highly preferred. Equivalent combination of advanced education and relevant experience will be considered.
* A minimum of 5 years of experience and deep knowledge in orchestration methods and tools to optimally use computational infrastructure, and machine learning methods and algorithms
* Strong Python or R programming skills required
* Experience developing, architecting, and running ML or deep learning workload in the Cloud.
* Ability to express the intuition behind basic ML algorithms
* Experience performing basic hyper parameter optimization
* Ability to follow model training, deployment, and operational best practices
* Experience with Docker for containerization and Kubernetes for orchestration
* Knowledge of Terraform and Ansible for automating infrastructure management
* Knowledge of YAML for automating model and application code deployment
* Understanding of data engineering principles and databases (relational, NoSQL)
* Strong critical thinking and communication skills
**Additional Desired Qualifications:**

* AWS certification is highly preferred, with a strong preference for the AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty credential.
* Experience with Bayesian statistical modeling, including Bayesian inference, Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC), and causal inference.
* Hands‑on experience in geospatial data analytics using Esri products, with a focus on ArcGIS Enterprise.

results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3049014
[...]
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
[...]
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date May 15, 2026
[...]
## Job Details
[...]
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
[...]
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
[...]
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
[...]
Key Responsibilities
[...]
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
[...]
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
[...]
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
[...]
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
[...]
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
[...]
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
[...]
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
[...]
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
[...]
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
[...]
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
[...]
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
[...]
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
[...]
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
[...]
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
[...]
Familiarity with cloud computing environments (AWS preferred), containerization (Docker), and CI/
[...]
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
[...]
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
[...]
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems

results.title: 212: I Quit My Six-Figure Data Science Job. Was It Worth It?
highlights: the end. I started my career as a chemical lab technician, and I absolutely hated my job. I learned about data science and got immediately hooked. I was like, "Wow, this is the coolest thing ever. I wanna become a data scientist." And I worked really hard to learn all the stuff I needed to learn to become a data scientist, and I ultimately landed this six-figure job at ExxonMobil, which utilized my chemistry background to be a data scientist. I moved from Utah to Texas to work at their headquarters there, and it was a great job. It was awesome. I got paid over six figures. I got a really cushy desk job. I literally never worked over 40 hours a week. Um, I liked my coworkers. I liked the campus that we were on. Everything was really great, and I had my dream data scientist life. And that's how everything was until it wasn't, and everything went wrong, and I was absolutely miserable. Although everything on the outside looked amazing, and it was, I really wasn't fulfilled at my job for a couple different reasons, but basically, I thought I was a really good contributor. I thought I had really good ideas. I thought I was bringing a lot to the table, and, uh, not a lot of that was getting reflected at the company, and I grew really tired and sick of it. For example, earlier, I had built an app that they, not me, had deemed worth over a billion dollars, and yes, that's B with a billion dollars, and I don't feel like I was getting the ownership or the credit that I did on this entire project. I had also started to post a lot on LinkedIn and become kind of a thought leader in terms of data analytics plus oil and gas, and I... My posts started getting recognized by people at Exxon. So for example, a VP of Exxon saw one of my posts, reached out to me, and was like, "Hey, I wanna talk to you about data analytics at ExxonMobil." And I said, "Great. My manager and I would love to do that with you," because keep in mind, I'm an individual contributor. I'm not a manager. I'm a nobody. And the VP says, "No, I don't want you to bring your manager. I just want you to come." And I said, "Okay." And so I told my manager, I'm like, "Hey, this VP wants to talk to me. I'm gonna go talk to him," and my manager wouldn't let me. And I was... felt like I was being trapped, basically, in my role, and that got really unfulfilling in this, like, corporate bureaucracy that I really just got sick of, and I knew I was miserable because I started tracking how happy I was to walk in the door every day. And instead of, you know, sevens and eights on how happy I was, it was, like, twos and threes, and at that point, I knew something had to change. But to what, I didn't really know. I was always interested in being an entrepreneur. I had tried to start some businesses in high school and college, and none of them had worked out whatsoever, but I was always fascinated by, you know, owning your own business and trying to do something on your own. But I'm, like, extremely risk adverse. I'm pretty cautious, and starting my own business seemed way too much of a risk for, for me to pursue. And honestly, I would have never have pursued it had it not been for my loving and supportive wife really encouraging me to try it out. She knew how much it meant to me. She thought I could do it, and she believed in me more than I believed in myself, and so with her confidence, I- I went ahead and I did this: Hey, what's up? And, um, I'm gonna be resigning from ExxonMobil. Um, it's a super hard decision. Um, I just think I, I see myself going in, in different directions than, than the company Um, yeah, it pretty much has. Okay, sounds good. Thanks. Bye I did it. Oh my gosh, that was so... Crazy. And you might have sensed a little bit of emotion at the end of that last clip, and it's because I had just done something that terrified me. I was really scared. I didn't know what I was doing. I didn't know how I was going to bring income to my family. I didn't know if this was gonna work.

results.title: How to Get an AI Engineer Job in Nigeria (Step-by-Step Guide) | 2026 | Profolio
highlights:
An AI Engineer designs, builds, and deploys artificial intelligence and machine learning systems that solve real-world problems. Unlike a data scientist who focuses primarily on experimentation and analysis, an AI Engineer takes models from research into production — building the infrastructure, pipelines, and APIs that allow AI systems to operate reliably at scale.
[...]
- AI Engineer
- Machine Learning Engineer
- MLOps Engineer
- NLP Engineer
- Computer Vision Engineer
- Data Scientist (with engineering focus)
- AI Research Engineer
- Deep Learning Engineer
- AI Solutions Engineer
- Applied AI Engineer
[...]
the institutions whose graduates
[...]
- Machine learning and deep learning. You need a solid understanding of supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning algorithms. For most roles, deep learning knowledge — including neural networks, CNNs, RNNs, and transformer architectures — is increasingly required, especially as large language models become central to AI products.
[...]
- MLOps and model deployment. Being able to train a model is not enough. Employers want engineers who can deploy models to production using tools like Docker, Kubernetes, FastAPI, Flask, and cloud platforms (AWS SageMaker, Google Vertex AI, or Azure ML). MLflow, Airflow, and similar pipeline tools are also valued.
[...]
- Recognised AI and ML certifications. While certifications do not replace skills, they signal structured learning and commitment. Valuable certifications for Nigerian AI Engineers include the Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer, AWS Certified Machine Learning Specialty, Microsoft Azure AI Engineer Associate, DeepLearning.AI specialisations (Coursera), and the fast.ai Practical Deep Learning course. These are widely recognised by Nigerian tech hiring managers.
[...]
Company career pages should be your primary application channel. Flutterwave, Paystack, Kuda Bank, MTN Nigeria, and Interswitch post AI roles on their official websites before aggregator sites. Subscribe to their job alerts and check career pages weekly. LinkedIn Jobs, Jobberman, and HotNigerianJobs are also useful for discovery, but prioritise direct applications where possible.
[...]
- Shell Nigeria
- TotalEnergies Nigeria
- Seplat Energy
- ExxonMobil Nigeria
- Oando
- Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC)
[...]
AI roles in oil and gas are fewer but exceptionally well-compensated. They focus on predictive maintenance, seismic interpretation, and operational optimisation. Most are based in Lagos or Port Harcourt.
[...]
The most active employers of AI Engineers in Nigeria are fintech companies (Flutterwave, Paystack, Kuda Bank, OPay, Carbon), traditional banks (GTBank, Access Bank, Zenith Bank), telecoms (MTN Nigeria, Airtel Nigeria), technology firms (Andela, Interswitch, Terragon), consulting firms (Deloitte Nigeria, Accenture), and oil and gas companies (Shell Nigeria, TotalEnergies, Seplat). Most AI hiring is concentrated in Lagos, with secondary markets in Abuja and Port Harcourt.
[...]
Python is non-negotiable and must be your primary language. Beyond Python, proficiency in SQL is expected for data handling. Key frameworks include TensorFlow or PyTorch (at least one deeply), Scikit-learn, Hugging Face Transformers for NLP work, and Pandas and NumPy for data manipulation. For deployment and MLOps, you should know Docker, FastAPI or Flask, at least one cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure), and Git for version control. Familiarity with Apache Airflow or similar pipeline orchestration tools is increasingly valued at mid to senior level. At Nigerian companies specifically, Google Cloud and AWS are the most commonly used cloud platforms.
[...]

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve complex problems in
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

results.title: Exxon Mobil AI Brand Visibility May, 2026
highlights:
Exxon Mobil AI Brand Visibility May,
[...]
# Exxon Mobil AI Brand Visibility (May, 2026)
[...]
AI Brand Visibility for Exxon Mobil (exxonmobil.com) reflects an extremely low level of exposure in AI results, where global energy production and distribution is the strongest entity with a Visibility Index of 62, typical rank around 1.0, and appearing in roughly 75% of AI replies. Across all 5 entities linked to Exxon Mobil, the average Visibility Index is about 25, with moderate LLM confidence at roughly 40% and limited brand discovery around 30%. For the top entity global energy production and distribution, AI responses show very strong confidence at about 100% and very strong brand match near 95%, indicating below target visibility quality for the strongest touchpoint. In addition, oil exploration and extraction services shows below target visibility, combining a Visibility Index of 62 with solid discovery near 75% and very strong confidence near 100%. Overall, the report highlights sizeable gaps in how Exxon Mobil is surfaced by large language models, underscoring the need for stronger brand signals and content aligned to AI discovery.
[...]

RankLens measures how visible a brand is inside large language models (LLMs). It runs controlled sampling of AI responses, tracks where and how often your brand is mentioned, and normalizes the results into a Visibility Index so you can benchmark and track AI brand presence over time. RankLens is based on Entity-Conditioned Probing + Resampling principles.
[...]
Rank (Avg) Average ranking position of the entity across all sampling iterations (lower is better). LLM Confidence The normalized confidence score the LLM reports for its answers including your brand. Brand Appearance (SoV) How many times the brand or website appears in the sampled responses out of total iterations. Share of Voice (SoV): % of all responses the AI assigned to a brand compared to all other brands. Brand Discovery The probability (as a percentage) that the brand appears in an LLM response for the tested entities. Brand Target A normalized measure based on confidence interval width showing how tightly focused the LLM responses are on the brand. Brand Match Accuracy of matching the results to the intended brand name or website. Visibility Index An overall score out of 100 combining probability, confidence, and targeting to summarize AI brand visibility.
[...]

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
highlights:
Machine Learning Scientist, Scientific Reasoning Models, AI for Drug Discovery – NLP People
[...]
Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche’s Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness the transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide. ​
[...]
At Roche’s AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group, we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning (ML) techniques. We are seeking a Machine Learning Scientist to join the Foundation Models team within Prescient Design (gRED). In this role, you will contribute to our internal reasoning Large Language Models (LLMs) and enable it to succeed at relevant drug discovery tasks, including biomolecular design. You will work at the intersection of engineering and research, designing and scaling large machine learning systems.
[...]
Scalable Systems & Engineering: Design, implement, and improve large-scale distributed machine learning systems, writing robust, performance-critical code and contributing to core infrastructure.
[...]
Model Improvement & Reasoning: Develop and execute strategies to systematically improve performance on scientific tasks, including long-horizon task completion and complex reasoning challenges.
[...]
Domain Translation: Translate biological and chemical domain knowledge into concrete machine learning objectives, training signals, and evaluation criteria.
[...]
Evaluation & Benchmarks: Design and implement evaluation methodologies to assess model capabilities relevant to biological research, working with domain experts to establish benchmarks and curate high-quality data.
[...]
Research-to-Production: Collaborate closely with researchers to translate ideas and prototypes into scalable, production-ready systems.
[...]
Machine Learning Scientist
[...]
Focus: You focus on the execution of defined projects. You are responsible for writing clean, efficient code to test specific hypotheses regarding reasoning and alignment.
[...]
Engineering: You contribute to the maintenance of the training infrastructure and data pipelines, ensuring experiments run reliably on our clusters.
[...]
Collaboration: You work closely with senior scientists to implement novel algorithms, translating research papers into working prototypes.
[...]
BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative field with 2+ years of relevant work experience. Or Ph. D. with 0-2 years relevant work experience.
[...]
LLM Expertise: Experience developing and training large-scale machine learning models, including post-training techniques to enhance domain knowledge, reasoning capabilities, and model alignment.
[...]
Experience with molecular modalities (e.g., protein sequences, chemical graphs, and structured molecular data).
[...]
A public portfolio of research or significant contributions to open-source ML libraries.
[...]
A passion for applying frontier AI to drug discovery.
[...]
